articles.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
articles.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

articles.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
articles.description: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

articles.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
articles.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

articles.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
articles.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

articles.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
articles.description: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

articles.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount’s $110B Warner Bros. deal
articles.description: The states allege that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

articles.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
articles.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

articles.title: As TV-tracking app TV Time shuts down, its founder builds Bingers, a new home for fans
articles.description: The creator of TV Time is building a successor app that will let users import their watch histories and preserve the community that formed around discussing their favorite shows.

articles.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
articles.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

articles.title: SpaceX cleared to fly Starship again after booster failure in May
articles.description: This will be the first Starship test flight for SpaceX as a public company, testing the market's appetite for the company's "fly, fail, fix" approach to rocket development, which often ends in fireballs.

articles.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
articles.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

articles.title: LAPD lets contract with surveillance giant Flock expire, citing ‘serious concerns’ over civil liberties and privacy
articles.description: The LAPD, one of Flock's biggest government customers, is ending its contract with the company citing civil liberties concerns.

articles.title: Uber’s robotaxi lobbying effort puts it on a collision course with Waymo
articles.description: Washington, D.C. has become a battleground for Uber and Waymo's competing views.

articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum
articles.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

articles.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
articles.description: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs' words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

articles.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
articles.description: Ninja's latest slushie machine builds on the popularity of the original Slushi, but with a big upgrade.

articles.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
articles.description: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
articles.description: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

articles.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
articles.description: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

articles.description: Ukraine’s drone blitz halted Russia’s Sea of Azov shipping in under a week.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **微软CEO纳德拉发出AI企业警告**：Satya Nadella对使用AI的公司发出严厉警告，提醒企业需谨慎应对AI带来的潜在风险与责任。
2. **苹果起诉前员工窃密跳槽OpenAI**：苹果指控一名前员工在离职加入OpenAI后，利用“罕见”系统漏洞下载公司机密文件，引发商业秘密诉讼。
3. **AI伦理争议：是否应助人“脱罪”**：一篇讨论AI是否应帮助用户逃避谋杀配偶法律责任的报道，引发对AI道德边界的激烈辩论。
4. **通用聚变公司上市首日大涨**：作为首家公开交易的聚变能源公司，General Fusion在上市首日股价飙升，标志核聚变商业化迈出重要一步。
5. **12州起诉阻止派拉蒙与华纳兄弟千亿合并**：美国12个州联合提起诉讼，试图阻止派拉蒙与华纳兄弟价值1100亿美元的合并案，反垄断监管趋严。

今日最值得关注的发展是苹果与OpenAI之间的商业秘密诉讼，揭示了科技巨头间人才争夺与知识产权保护的尖锐冲突。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **Hugging Face多领域更新**：发布PyTorch性能分析（Attention机制）第三部分、AI代理数据方案、vLLM原生速度Transformer后端、一键部署至Amazon SageMaker Studio、Foundry托管计算支持、SkyPilot零出站存储多云运行、以及机器人学习库LeRobot v0.6.0（含想象、评估、改进功能）。

2. **OpenAI与Google AI产品迭代**：Google Gemini API扩展托管代理（支持后台任务、远程MCP）；OpenAI推出GPT-5.6作为Microsoft 365 Copilot首选模型、启动GPT-5.5生物漏洞赏金计划，并强调ChatGPT成为“最雄心勃勃工作的合作伙伴”。

3. **行业应用与安全合作**：德国电信利用AI重构通信网络（OpenAI博客）；OpenAI明确政府与国家安全伙伴合作策略，并发布ChatGPT入门指南。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI正式将GPT-5.6定位为“前沿智能”并嵌入Microsoft 365 Copilot，标志着AI助手向企业级核心工作流的深度渗透。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **科技与工业突破**：比亚迪在全球电动汽车竞争中超越特斯拉，中国在极紫外光刻技术（EUV）研发上取得接近突破的进展，并首次成功实现轨道火箭回收，同时发布地球“准卫星”Kamo'oalewa的首张照片。

2. **资源与产业竞争**：印度初创企业试图通过软件打破中国对稀土资源的垄断；中国已成为全球最大鹅肝生产国，超越法国。

3. **就业与社会问题**：中国面临毕业生就业压力，大量高学历人才进入需求有限的就业市场；同时，中国本土也出现了类似“男性圈”的性别议题现象，以及Z世代自由职业者“无老板、无收入”的生存现状。

4. **国际关系与舆论博弈**：中俄在人工智能数据中心议题上联合推动争议讨论，并加强“无限制”伙伴关系；西方媒体对中国科技成就存在不实报道，部分舆论试图以“但中国”为借口转移焦点。

5. **互联网治理与技术细节**：详细披露了中国互联网防火墙的技术运作机制，显示中国在网络主权管理上的持续强化。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中国在电动汽车、光刻技术、航天回收及深空探测等领域集中取得显著突破，体现出科技自主创新的加速推进。
